story_clusters.summary: A plea has been filed in the Supreme Court seeking a probe into the activities of the Cockroach Janta Party and the alleged use of fake law degrees by its members. The Supreme Court declined an urgent hearing of the plea, with the Chief Justice of India advising the complainant not to take the matter "so sentimentally." Multiple sources report that the plea requests a CBI investigation into these issues.

story_clusters.summary: The U.S. and Iran are making progress toward a potential deal, though key differences remain over uranium enrichment and sanctions relief. Oil prices have fallen amid optimism about a U.S.-Iran agreement, while Iran has rejected remarks by Senator Marco Rubio and accused U.S. sanctions of disrupting global energy markets. Separately, India and the U.S. are working toward finalizing a trade deal, with an American delegation expected to visit Delhi.

articles.title: Assam introduces Uniform Civil Code bill in Assembly
articles.summary: The Assam government has introduced a Uniform Civil Code bill in the state Assembly. The proposed law aims to ban polygamy and mandate the registration of live-in relationships. The bill will be discussed in the Assembly on Wednesday. The legislation could have wide-ranging implications for personal laws and social practices in the state.
articles.raw_summary: The proposed legislation, which seeks to ban polygamy and make registration of live-in relationships compulsory, will be taken up for discussion on Wednesday.

articles.summary: The Assam government has tabled the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in the state assembly. The bill is scheduled for discussion and passage on May 27. The verdict is a legal action with implications for gender rights and welfare policies in India.
articles.raw_summary: Both are practices shaped by patriarchy and religion. But one is determined by individual autonomy and the other by biology.

articles.title: NCLAT refuses to condone 1-day delay beyond statutory 45-day limit under Section 61(2) IBC despite e-filing portal glitches
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) refused to condone a one-day delay in filing beyond the statutory 45-day limit under Section 61(2) of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), even due to technical glitches in the e-filing portal. The tribunal emphasized that it cannot exceed its jurisdiction as defined by the legislature, stating that granting such condonation would undermine legislative intent. The ruling underscores the strict adherence to statutory timelines in insolvency
articles.raw_summary: NCLAT observed that Tribunal cannot clothe itself with a jurisdiction which has not been vested upon it by the legislature. Thus, grant condonation of delay beyond the 15th day even on equitable consideration or on the ground of hardship or technical considerations will amount to jettisoning the intent of the legislature which is impermissible. articles.title: Section 271(1)(c) Penalty cannot continue after Assessee’s Death
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that penalty proceedings under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act cannot continue against the legal heirs of a deceased taxpayer. The Tribunal held that legal representatives are not personally liable for such penalties under Section 159 of the Act. This decision clarifies the scope of tax liability for heirs and has implications for tax enforcement and compliance in India.
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT held that penalty proceedings under Section 271(1)(c) cannot continue against legal heirs when initiated after the assessee’s demise. The Tribunal ruled that legal representatives are not personally liable for such penalty proceedings under Section 159. articles.title: Buying Flats in Others’ Names With Self-Funded Consideration Is Benami Transaction: SAFEMA
articles.summary: The SAFEMA Appellate Tribunal ruled that purchasing flats in someone else's name using one's own funds constitutes a benami transaction under the amended Benami Act. The tribunal held that this practice falls under Section 2(9)(A) of the Act, where the payment is made by one person but the property is held by another. This decision clarifies the legal stance on such transactions and has implications for property buyers and sellers in India.

Tribunal ruled that payment of consideration by one person while property is held by another attracts Section 2(9)(A) of amended Benami Act. articles.title: ITAT Upholds Section 263 Revision as AO Verified Expenses Only on Random Basis
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) upheld a revision under Section 263 of the Income Tax Act, stating that the Assessing Officer (AO) had only verified expenses randomly and insufficiently. The Tribunal ruled that random verification was inadequate, especially for substantial cash expenses lacking proper vouchers. This decision reinforces the need for thorough scrutiny of expense claims in tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: Pune ITAT upheld revision under Section 263 after finding that the Assessing Officer failed to fully verify expenditure claims unsupported by vouchers. The Tribunal held that random verification was insufficient where substantial cash expenses were claimed. The post ITAT Upholds Section 263 Revision as AO Verified Expenses Only on Random Basis appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Declared Commission Income Cannot Be Ignored While Examining Cash Deposits: ITAT Agra
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Agra ruled that cash deposits in a taxpayer's account cannot be treated as unexplained income if the taxpayer has already declared commission income in their tax return. The tribunal granted relief to the assessee after finding that the tax officer failed to consider the declared income source while examining the cash deposits. This decision underscores the importance of assessing declared income before treating deposits as unexplained.

articles.title: Agricultural Land Also Covered Under Section 56(2)(x): ITAT Surat
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Surat ruled that agricultural land falls under the definition of 'immovable property' as per Section 56(2)(x) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal stated that purchases of such land below stamp duty valuation may attract tax on the differential amount. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of agricultural land transactions and could impact future tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Surat held that agricultural land qualifies as “immovable property” under Section 56(2)(x) since the provision covers “any land or building or both.” The Tribunal ruled that purchases below stamp duty valuation may attract tax on the differential amount. articles.title: ITAT Allows Telescoping as Income Already Declared Cannot Be Taxed Again as Commission
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that income already declared in tax returns cannot be taxed again as commission, restricting 'telescoping' to net disclosed income. The decision clarifies that once transactions are deemed bogus, expenses reflected in gross profit cannot be separately allowed. This ruling impacts tax assessment practices and provides clarity on income declaration rules.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that once transactions are treated as bogus, there is no basis for separately allowing expenses reflected in gross profit. Telescoping was therefore restricted to net income disclosed in the return. articles.title: GST Assessment Against Legal Heirs Set Aside Due to Lack of Personal Hearing: Rajasthan HC
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court set aside GST assessment proceedings against the legal heirs of a deceased assessee due to the absence of a mandatory personal hearing. The court ruled that assessment proceedings against legal representatives must comply with Section 75 of the GST law, and any order passed without a personal hearing is invalid. This decision impacts how tax authorities conduct assessments involving deceased taxpayers' estates.

articles.title: Govt relaxes insolvency rules for MSMEs
articles.summary: The Indian government has relaxed insolvency rules for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to simplify the process. Starting in 2026, only one valuer will be appointed per asset class to reduce costs, and strict new regulations will prevent conflicts of interest in pre-packaged resolutions. Related parties, recent auditors, and associates of resolution professionals are now barred from appointment.
articles.raw_summary: New rules aim to simplify insolvency for MSMEs. Only one valuer will be appointed per asset class, cutting costs. Strict new regulations prevent conflicts of interest in pre-packaged resolutions. Related parties, recent auditors, and associates of resolution professionals are now barred from appointment. These changes are effective from 2026.

articles.title: Huawei proposes new path for chip development amid US sanctions
articles.summary: Huawei is working on a new chip design strategy called the 'Tau Scaling Law' to improve semiconductor performance by 2031. The company aims to bypass US sanctions that restrict access to advanced chip technology. Huawei has already produced hundreds of chips using this approach and plans to integrate it into its upcoming Kirin chips with a 'LogicFolding' architecture. This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's own policy on domestic chip manufacturing.
articles.raw_summary: Huawei aims for advanced chip design by 2031. The company is developing a new 'Tau Scaling Law' to improve chip performance. This innovation could bypass US sanctions restricting access to cutting-edge semiconductor technology. Huawei's upcoming Kirin chips will feature a related 'LogicFolding' architecture. The company has already produced hundreds of chips based on this new principle.

articles.title: No Financial Debt Without Disbursement of Money to Corporate Debtor Under IBC
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) ruled that disbursement of funds to a corporate debtor is mandatory for a claim to qualify as financial debt under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC). In this case, since no money was advanced to the debtor, the claimant was not recognized as a financial creditor. This decision reinforces the legal threshold for financial debt claims in insolvency cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal reiterated that disbursement of funds to the debtor is an essential condition for financial debt. Since no money was advanced to the corporate debtor, the claimant was not a financial creditor. The post No Financial Debt Without Disbursement of Money to Corporate Debtor Under IBC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: PBPT Act Does Not Permit Attachment of Abettor’s Assets Without Benami Finding: SAFEMA
articles.summary: The SAFEMA Appellate Tribunal ruled that properties belonging to an alleged abettor cannot be attached under the Prevention of Benami Property Transactions (PBPT) Act unless those properties are independently proven to be benami. The Tribunal clarified that the Act only permits attachment of benami properties held by a benamidar or beneficial owner. This ruling impacts how benami property cases are handled under the law.

articles.title: Foreign Liabilities and Assets (FLA) Return
articles.summary: The Foreign Liabilities and Assets (FLA) Return must be filed by entities in India if they hold foreign assets or liabilities as of March 31, even without new transactions. This requirement is part of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) reporting norms to ensure transparency in outstanding Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) and Overseas Direct Investment (ODI) positions. Failure to comply may lead to regulatory action.
articles.raw_summary: The requirement applies if foreign assets or liabilities exist as of 31 March, even without fresh transactions. The rule ensures disclosure of outstanding FDI/ODI positions under FEMA reporting norms. The post Foreign Liabilities and Assets (FLA) Return appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CCI Rejects Aftermarket Abuse Theory in Private Hospital Pricing Investigation
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) rejected the theory that private hospitals abuse their market power in pricing for in-patient services. The CCI found that patients receive cost estimates before elective treatments and can choose alternative hospitals, concluding that in-patient services should not be treated as a separate aftermarket under competition law. The decision impacts how healthcare pricing and competition are regulated in India.
articles.raw_summary: Commission found that patients undergoing elective treatment generally receive estimated treatment costs in advance and retain the ability to choose alternative hospitals. It therefore declined to treat in-patient services as a separate aftermarket under competition law. The post CCI Rejects Aftermarket Abuse Theory in Private Hospital Pricing Investigation appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CCI Closes Hospital Overpricing Case as Excessive Pricing Was Not Established
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) closed a case against hospitals for alleged overpricing after finding that the investigation failed to establish excessive pricing. The CCI noted that the benchmarks used, such as standalone labs and hotels, were inadequate and did not compare hospital charges with similarly placed hospitals. The decision highlights the challenges in proving unfair pricing under competition law.
articles.raw_summary: The CCI found that the investigation failed to compare hospital charges with similarly placed hospitals and relied on inadequate benchmarks such as standalone labs and hotels. The Commission therefore held that unfair pricing was not conclusively proved. The post CCI Closes Hospital Overpricing Case as Excessive Pricing Was Not Established appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The article compares how Nepal and Sri Lanka reformed their political systems in response to public uprisings, while Bangladesh suppressed opposition and declared victory. It highlights governance approaches to civil unrest and political reform, offering lessons for regional democracies. The analysis focuses on party politics and institutional responses, with potential implications for India's own political and policy landscape.
articles.raw_summary: Nepal and Sri Lanka responded to comparable uprisings by redesigning the rules of the game. Bangladesh removed the opposing team from the field and called it a victory.

The move addresses concerns about underage users on social media and could reignite debates about stricter regulations or bans for teenage social media use. The initiative raises questions about data privacy and child safety, which are key policy concerns in India.

articles.title: Government expands fuel dispenser verification to CNG, LNG, Hydrogen as clean energy push gathers pace
articles.summary: The Indian government has expanded the scope of Government Approved Test Centres (GATC) to verify and re-verify fuel dispensers for CNG, LNG, and Hydrogen, adding to the existing 23 categories under the Legal Metrology framework. This move aims to ensure accurate fuel delivery and greater transparency in transactions as the country pushes for cleaner energy adoption. The decision comes amid increasing use of alternative fuels and aligns with broader clean energy initiatives.
articles.raw_summary: With the inclusion of these instruments, Government Approved Test Centres can now undertake verification and re-verification of a total of 23 categories of weights and measures under the Legal Metrology framework. The inclusion of CNG, LNG and Hydrogen dispensers under the GATC framework comes at a time when the use of cleaner fuels is increasing, with the measure aiming for accurate fuel delivery and greater transparency in transactions.

articles.title: Additions for Unaccounted Sales Cannot Be Based on Mere Suspicion or Estimated Production Yield: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that tax authorities cannot add unaccounted sales or suppressed production to a taxpayer's books based solely on suspicion or estimated production yields. The court upheld that such additions require tangible supporting evidence. This decision reinforces the need for concrete data in tax assessments and sets a precedent for future tax disputes in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court upheld the finding that rejection of books and addition for suppressed production were invalid where the Assessing Officer relied only on estimated yield comparisons. The ruling reiterates that tax assessments require tangible supporting material. The post Additions for Unaccounted Sales Cannot Be Based on Mere Suspicion or Estimated Production Yield: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

The discussion stems from a case highlighting the need for transparency in real estate transactions.

articles.summary: Brent and WTI crude oil prices fell by around 6% after reports of a potential US-Iran peace deal, with Brent crude dropping to $97.69 per barrel and WTI to $90.85 per barrel. India, a major oil importer, may see indirect impacts on fuel prices and inflation.

This decision highlights procedural lapses in tax appeals and reinforces the need for fair adjudication in tax disputes.

articles.title: CCI Rejects Excessive Pricing Allegations Against Super-Specialty Hospital for Lack of Comparable Evidence
articles.summary: The Competition Commission of India (CCI) dismissed allegations of excessive pricing against a private super-specialty hospital, stating there was insufficient evidence to prove the pricing was both excessive and unfair. The hospital had been accused of charging higher prices for tests, medicines, and consumables. The CCI's decision highlights the need for comparable evidence in such cases. This ruling sets a precedent for future competition law enforcement in the healthcare sector.
articles.raw_summary: The CCI held that higher prices charged by a private super-specialty hospital for tests, medicines, and consumables did not automatically amount to abuse of dominance. The Commission found no sufficient evidence proving that the pricing was both excessive and unfair. articles.title: Opportunity To Lead Pre-Summoning Evidence Must Be Given Even In Criminal Cases Appearing 'Civil': Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that even where a Magistrate declines to order registration of an FIR under Section 156(3) CrPC, the complainant must ordinarily be given an opportunity to lead pre-summoning evidence before the complaint is dismissed as being “purely civil” in nature. Justice Manoj Jain observed that the “dividing line” between a civil breach of contract and criminality is often “a thin one,” and therefore the complainant should be allowed to establish the alleged criminal offence

articles.summary: Aluminium prices are near multi-year highs due to persistent supply shortages driven by stricter environmental regulations, high energy costs, and production limits. Global inventories are low, further tightening supply and sustaining elevated prices. The rally is attributed to structural factors rather than temporary disruptions.

Stricter environmental rules, high energy costs, and production caps are limiting output, while low global inventories further tighten supply and support elevated prices.

articles.title: AI Promises Efficiency but the Fashion Industry Needs a Political Reckoning
articles.summary: Fashion for Good's report outlines a vision for an AI-driven fashion supply chain by 2036, emphasizing technical feasibility and operational seriousness. However, it raises unaddressed concerns about labour shifts, legal responsibility, and regulatory gaps that could either drive progress or perpetuate existing systemic failures. The discussion underscores the need for policy frameworks to manage AI's societal and economic impacts in industries like fashion.
articles.raw_summary: Ten years is a long horizon in any industry, but in AI it may be a category error. Fashion for Good's mapping of a 2036 AI-native supply chain is technically grounded and operationally serious. The structural questions it leaves unanswered, around rebound effects, labour transition, legal accountability, and regulatory reach, are the ones that will determine whether the vision it describes produces genuine progress or faster versions of the same systemic failures.

articles.title: Bellatrix readying ultra-low orbit satellites
articles.summary: Bellatrix, a Bengaluru-based company, is developing ultra-low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellites with targeted launches in 2027 or 2028. This project aims to address challenges posed by extreme atmospheric drag in very low orbits, an area currently underexplored globally. The initiative could position India as a leader in advanced satellite operations, potentially influencing future space policy and regulatory frameworks.
articles.raw_summary: The Bengaluru-based company is targeting launches in 2027 or 2028 and is positioning the project as a potential breakthrough in very low Earth orbit (VLEO) satellite operations, an area that remains largely unexplored globally because of the extreme atmospheric drag at those altitudes.

articles.title: I4C warns of phishing scam targeting iPhone users via fake Apple support messages
articles.summary: The Indian Cyber Crime Coordination Centre (I4C) has warned iPhone users about a phishing scam where scammers impersonate Apple Support to steal Apple IDs and OTPs via malicious links. Victims risk losing access to their devices and accounts. The scam underscores the need for vigilance and the use of Apple's official 'Find Devices' service for recovery.
articles.raw_summary: Beware of a cunning phishing scam targeting iPhone users who have lost their devices. Scammers, posing as Apple Support, are sending fake messages with malicious links to steal your Apple ID and OTP. This allows them to access your account and remove your device. Digital adoption remains high among these businesses.
